title: [CoreToFSM] Fix concat-in-mux losing possible state values
body:
The `generateConcatenatedValues` helper in the CoreToFSM pass used `std::move` to assign its computed results into `finalPossibleValues`, which overwrote any values that had already been accumulated by earlier branches of a mux. This caused the BFS next-state analysis to silently drop reachable states when a `comb.mux` had one constant branch and one `comb.concat` branch. The fix replaces the move-assignment with an insert loop so that previously accumulated values are preserved.

A regression test (`concat-mux.mlir`) is included. It exercises a module whose next-state logic is `mux(b, 3, concat(a, 0))`, which has three reachable state values (0, 3, 4). Without the fix the pass only discovers two of them (0 and 4) because the constant 3 from the mux's true branch is overwritten when processing the concat on the false branch.
